title: Accelerating Quantum Computing Research Using AI and Robotics
description: Chapter 3 of dossier QC-Accelerate-002. Chapter 2 mapped the closed loop and named the race; this chapter places the bets. Not which platform wins — a question it refuses on purpose — but two answerable ones: where the decide-make-measure-learn cycle plausibly closes first on qubits (the venue bet: defect and materials qubits, where machine-steered synthesis, feedback-stop defect writing, and 4-kelvin operation already exist separately), and what to build that pays off regardless of the winner (the platform-portable loop stack, plus a five-platform portfolio with dated entry and exit signposts, each lane drawn at its evidence grade). A bounded funding corollary prices the one ask the evidence licenses: America's answer to Hefei's co-location has already broken ground on a dead steel mill — what no public document funds is the loop layer, and the chapter says so with a date and a percentage. Every bet carries a deadline a hostile referee can score; two living figures — a hollow scoreboard built to be filled in, and a cell-by-cell gap map drawn only from the dossier's own citations — make the bets and the evidence grades visible. An Open Dossier publication: executable claims under CI, published adversarial review, and OpenTimestamps-anchored releases.
